findings.explanation: Interprocedural leak: `session_open()` returns the allocated `session`; ownership transfers to its caller, which must free it. The caller was not found in this file. The allocation at /Users/zed/Master/leak-investigator/demo/memory_leak_corpus/ownership_maze/session.c:8 (`session`) has no matching free on the leaking path.
findings.repair_suggestion: Ensure the object allocated via malloc in session_open is released on every exit path. Add cleanup before each return or route ownership to a caller that clearly frees it.

root_cause.description: the function transfers ownership by returning the allocation, but the caller never frees it
root_cause.rootCauseDescription: `session_open()` returns the allocated `session`; ownership transfers to its caller, which must free it. The caller was not found in this file.
